source: With a Little Help from My Friends- Nearest-Neighbor Contrastive Learning of Visual Representations.pdf

concepts.finding:
Next we introduce our approach, Nearest-Neighbor
Contrastive Learning of visual Representations (NNCLR),
which proposes using nearest-neighbours (NN) as positives
to improve contrastive instance discrimination methods.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
We make the following contributions: (i) We introduce
NNCLR to learn self-supervised representations that go be-
yond single instance positives, without resorting to cluster-
ing; (ii) We demonstrate that NNCLR increases the perfor-
mance of contrastive learning methods (e.g.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
SimCLR [11])
by ∼3.8% and achieves state of the art performance
on ImageNet classiﬁcation for linear evaluation and semi-
supervised setup with limited labels; (iii) Our method out-
performs state of the art methods on self-supervised, and
even supervised features (learned via supervised ImageNet
pr
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
On transfer learning benchmarks
our method outperforms state-of-the-art methods (including
supervised learning with ImageNet) on 8 out of 12 down-
stream datasets.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

trastive learning (row 2) is 3% better in Top-1 accuracy than

embedding size as shown in Table 7d.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Linear evaluation on ImageNet classiﬁcation
shows that it takes about 120 epochs for NNCLR to start
outperforming SimCLR, and remains higher until the end
of pre-traininig at 1000 epochs.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
We ﬁnd that using the nearest-neighbor as positive
in contrastive losses improves performance signiﬁcantly
on ImageNet classiﬁcation, from 71.7% to 75.6%, out-
performing previous state-of-the-art methods.
concepts.implication: Relevant for similarity-based retrieval and lookalike targeting

concepts.finding:
Furthermore, we demonstrate empirically
that our method is less reliant on complex data augmenta-
tions.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
In this work, we show how an abil-
ity to ﬁnd similarities across items within previously seen
examples improves the performance of self-supervised rep-
resentation learning.
concepts.implication: Relevant for similarity-based retrieval and lookalike targeting

concepts.finding:
However, instead of clustering or
learning prototypes, we maintain a support set of image em-
beddings and using nearest neighbors from that set to deﬁne
positive samples.
concepts.implication: Applicable to user/item clustering for personalization
concepts.open_question:
How to address the limitation described: However, instead of clustering or
learning prototypes, we maintain a support set of image em-
beddings and using nearest neighbors from that set to deﬁne
positive samples.

concepts.finding:
Unlike [8, 57, 63], our support set
is continually refreshed with new embeddings and we do
not maintain running averages of the embeddings.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Nearest neigh-
bor search has been an important tool across a wide range of
computer vision applications [14, 17, 30, 31, 50, 56], from
image retrieval to unsupervised feature learning.
concepts.implication: Relevant for similarity-based retrieval and lookalike targeting

concepts.finding:
[52] propose a method to learn landmarks on ob-
jects in an unsupervised manner by using nearest-neighbors
from other images of the same object while [19] show un-
supervised learning of action phases by using soft nearest-
neighbors across videos of the same action.
concepts.implication: Relevant for similarity-based retrieval and lookalike targeting

concepts.finding:
In contrast, in this work
we use nearest neighbor retrieval within a single modality
(RGB images), and we maintain an explicit support set of
prior embeddings to increase diversity.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:

supervised features to other downstream datasets and tasks.
